title: [circt-reduce] Add FIRRTL LayerDisable reduction
body:
Add a reduction which tries to specialize-disable all layers in a circuit.
This will attempt to specialize each layer away individually, by using
each layer as a separate match.

This can be a very juicy reduction as it can remove large swaths of the
circuit if layer code is not required for the test to pass.

### Reviewer Notes

The one thing I'm not sure about here is the `pm->run()` operating on the parent
op.  Maybe there is a cleaner way to do this?

### Testing

I tested this on a somewhat recalcitrant test case and this got an 81% reduction after all other reductions had been tried:

```
# circt-reduce -test test.sh -keep-best -emit-bytecode design.anno.6.ir-fir.mlirbc -o design.anno.7.ir-fir.mlirbc -include firrtl-layer-disable
Reading input
Testing input with `test.sh`
Assertion failed: (isFullyEvaluated()), function finalize, file Evaluator.cpp, line 51.
Initial module has size 485779
Trying reduction `firrtl-layer-disable`
  [1/1; 100%; 2 ops, 2 at once; firrtl-layer-disable]
- Accepting module of size 95795
- No more ops where the pattern applies
All reduction strategies exhausted
Final size: 95795 (81% reduction)
```
